title: audit — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by AuditServiceProvider::boot(). All rules used by the AuditRetentionPolicyObserver chain (authoritative refusal on write) AND by FormRequest data classes (early request-time validation with clean error surfaces). Audit rows themselves are validated exclusively at the observer level — there is no HTTP entry point to attach FormRequest validators to.

rules.name: valid_retention_years
rules.description: Value is an integer in [1..config.audit.retention.max_years]. Default max is 10. Cap is configurable but never exceeds a hard-coded 25-year ceiling (the audit-log absolute retention limit before the platform requires a separate storage strategy).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be an integer between 1 and :max (inclusive).

rules.name: valid_override_dates
rules.description: override_starts_at is a valid ISO 8601 timestamp AND (override_ends_at IS NULL OR override_ends_at > override_starts_at). starts_at defaults to now() when omitted. ends_at NULL = indefinite.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must have starts_at < ends_at (or ends_at = null for indefinite).

rules.name: valid_event_type
rules.description: Value is one of the vendor-recognized types ('created', 'updated', 'deleted', 'restored') OR one of the module's registered custom_events (loaded from data/audited-events-catalog.json at boot). Custom events registered via vendor's `Auditable::registerAuditableTransition` seam appear in the catalog automatically at first invocation.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid audit event type. See data/audited-events-catalog.json for the full list.

rules.name: valid_auditable_type_exists
rules.description: Value is a class name that resolves at boot (via class_exists check) AND that class composes the vendor `Auditable` trait. Used when filtering audits by auditable_type — refuses filter values that would return empty because the type isn't a valid audited model.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a fully-qualified class name of an audited model.

rules.name: valid_override_reason_shape
rules.description: Value is a non-empty string, 10-500 chars. Reason must be descriptive enough to justify the retention extension to a compliance auditor reading the trail two years later. Very short reasons ('yes') fail; long structured reasons ('regulator SEC 2026-Q3 investigation, expected duration 18 months, contact compliance@tenant.example') pass.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a descriptive string, 10-500 characters. Vague reasons like 'yes' fail the shape check.
